lead_headline: Metro restaurant scene hits pause: zero openings, zero closures across the board for first time in weeks.

restaurant.blurb: Zero openings, zero closures over the past seven days — a rare flat week for Denver's 5,880-venue dining landscape. Last week saw six status changes including Riot House at Union Station and Reunion Bread in Platt Park, but the market has gone quiet. Whether it's post-winter recalibration or pre-spring lull, the numbers tell a story of collective breath-holding.

foreclosure.blurb: Zero new filings this week and through March so far — continuing February's momentum when six venues including Yogurtland Arvada and Cookie Dough Bliss surfaced on our radar. The 1,539-property database holds steady with no distress signals emerging from the residential side either. Quiet books across the board.

retail.blurb: Retail leasing and vacancy tracker in development — testing data feeds from CoStar and Placer.ai integrations.

revenue.blurb: Sales tax revenue analysis tool planned — coordinating municipal data partnerships with Denver, Aurora, Lakewood.

reputation.blurb: Review sentiment tracker planned — aggregating Google, Yelp, and inspection data for business health scoring.
